 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.5","title":"VFX determinism: golden hash registry + frame suite","description":"Background
- Performance optimizations require ironclad evidence that outputs are unchanged.
- Current VFX tests do not lock a canonical per-frame hash suite for each effect.

Goals
- Establish a golden hash registry for VFX effects, covering a deterministic frame suite.
- Make hash verification a standard gate for performance work.

Scope / Work Items
- Define a deterministic frame suite per effect (e.g., frames 0..5) using fixed seed/time/size.
- Capture per-frame hashes and store them via existing test infra (insta snapshots or existing hash registry).
- Extend e2e harness to validate hash sequences and report first mismatch clearly.
- Add a bless workflow for intentional changes with explicit rationale.

Constraints
- No quality/resolution reductions.
- Deterministic environment: fixed size, fixed tick cadence, fixed seed/time.
- Avoid new files unless required by snapshot tooling.

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests (ftui-demo-showcase) for hash sequences per effect.
- E2E run verifies all hashes and logs effect, frame_idx, hash, seed/time.
- Any mismatch fails fast with expected vs actual hash in logs.

Dependencies
- Requires deterministic seed/time control (bd-3isi7) and the VFX harness (bd-3e1t.5.1).","acceptance_criteria":"1) Golden hash registry covers at least metaballs, plasma, doom, quake, and one fractal effect.
2) Hashes are validated in CI via snapshot/e2e tests with clear failure output.
3) Hash logs include effect, frame_idx, hash, seed/time, and size.
4) Bless workflow exists for intentional changes with documented justification.","notes":"Added VFX golden hash registry + frame suite (metaballs/plasma/doom/quake/mandelbrot) with BLESS flow and note; vfx_frame logs now include seed/time/size/tick; generated golden checksums via BLESS; added deterministic harness validation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:57.405356220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:24:11.006302023Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:24:11.006234205Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:57.405356220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:58:07.626473430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3isi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:58:13.632107630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.7","title":"VFX perf baseline + flamegraph capture pack","description":"Background
- We need a stable, repeatable VFX performance baseline before any optimization.
- Flamegraphs should be anchored to deterministic runs so hotspots are comparable across effects.

Goals
- Capture baseline p50/p95/p99 total_ms for a standard matrix of effects and sizes.
- Produce flamegraphs for the heaviest effects to drive the hotspot matrix.

Scope / Work Items
- Define a benchmark matrix: effects (plasma, metaballs, doom, quake, fractal1), sizes (80x24, 120x40), frames (e.g., 120), tick_ms (e.g., 16), seed/time fixed.
- Use the VFX harness + perf timing to emit JSONL logs per run.
- Run hyperfine for each effect/size pair to reduce noise and report p50/p95/p99 total_ms.
- Capture flamegraphs via cargo flamegraph for the top 3 effects (per baseline).
- Summarize results in bead notes with run commands and metrics (no new files unless required by tooling).

Constraints
- No output changes; determinism required.
- Avoid script-based code changes; run commands manually.

Tests / Validation
- JSONL logs parse with schema validator; frame_idx monotonic and hashes stable.
- Baseline summary includes size, effect, frames, seed, tick_ms, p50/p95/p99.

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.6","title":"Dirty-span tracking for sparse diff (interval union)","description":"Background: diff benches show compute_dirty is often ~equal to compute for 1-row and ~5% sparse edits at 200x60 and 240x80; dirty rows are too coarse and still scan full rows. Goal: track per-row dirty spans (interval union) so diff scans only changed ranges while preserving exact output. Scope: add span storage to Buffer, merge policy, overflow fallback to full-row scan, and deterministic scan order identical to current row-major changes list. Constraints: no unsafe; no per-cell allocations; span storage O(rows)+O(spans); maintain dirty-soundness (if any cell in row y changed, at least one span covers it); must not change observable rendering output. Logging: emit evidence log fields for span_count, span_coverage, fallback_reason, and scan_cost_estimate. Tests: unit tests for merge/overflow and randomized diff-equivalence; property-based tests for edge cases (u16::MAX, empty rows, full rows). E2E: extend scripts/e2e_test.sh to run a sparse-update scenario with span path enabled and JSONL evidence logs; failure should report first mismatched frame with span diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"Diff output is bit-identical to compute across all cases; diff_bench shows measurable improvement on sparse (<=5%) updates and 1-row edits at large sizes; span tracking overhead stays <2% for dense workloads; evidence logs include span stats and fallback reasons; e2e scripts produce deterministic logs and fail loudly on mismatch.","notes":"Planning-first emphasis: this epic exists to convert coarse dirty rows into precise dirty spans so sparse edits scale with changed area, not screen area. Bench context (2026-02-04): diff/sparse_5pct/compute/200x60 ~21.8 us; compute_dirty ~= compute.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:40.638995632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:04:03.659648489Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:04:03.659628250Z","close_reason":"Dirty-span tracking appears fully implemented (buffer span tracking + diff span scan + logs + tests + e2e/bench children closed)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:43:40.638995632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","title":"Spec: dirty-span model, invariants, and merge policy","description":"Background: dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates. Task: specify per-row span data model, merge/union policy, max span cap, overflow fallback to full-row scan, deterministic ordering, and reset semantics via clear_dirty. Considerations: scissor/opacity interactions, optional guard bands, config defaults, and evidence log fields. Output: written spec captured in notes.","notes":"Dirty-Span Model (Spec v1)

Motivation
- Dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates: scanning full rows wastes work.
- Dirty-span tracking records per-row modified x-ranges to reduce diff scan cost while preserving soundness.

Data Model
- For each row y, maintain a small ordered list of spans: Span { x0: u16, x1: u16 } half-open.
- Spans are non-overlapping, sorted by x0 ascending.
- Per-row full_row/overflow flag indicates the row should be scanned fully; when true, spans list is empty.
- Optional guard band (merge_gap) merges spans that are within ≤ merge_gap cells.

Invariants (Must Hold)
1) Soundness: for any cell (x,y) mutated since last clear, ∃ span in row y such that x ∈ [x0,x1).
2) Sorted + non-overlapping spans per row (after merge).
3) Deterministic merge order: given the same mutation sequence, span lists are identical.
4) Full-row flag dominates: if full_row[y] = true, spans[y] is empty and row is scanned fully.

Merge / Union Policy
- Insert span (x0,x1) into row y:
  - If full_row[y] => no-op (already full).
  - Insert at position by x0 (binary search recommended).
  - Merge with neighbors when overlaps or when gap ≤ merge_gap.
  - After merge, if span_count > max_spans_per_row => set full_row[y]=true and clear spans.
- Default implementation values (current): merge_gap = 1, max_spans_per_row = 64.
- Optional future: make these configurable; add coverage threshold to flip to full_row.

Mutation API (Conceptual)
- mark_dirty_span(y, x0, x1)
- mark_dirty_cell(y, x) => mark_dirty_span(y, x, x+1)
- mark_dirty_rect(rect): for each row in rect, call mark_dirty_span(y, rect.x, rect.right())

Reset Semantics
- clear_dirty_spans(): clears all spans and full_row flags.
- On Buffer::new, initialize all rows as full_row to guarantee first diff is full.

Interaction with Existing Dirty Rows
- Dirty-span is a strict refinement; dirty_rows is a coarse fallback.
- If both exist, diff strategy may choose:
  - full_row[y] => full scan (row-skip still allowed)
  - spans[y].is_empty() => skip row
  - spans[y] => scan only spans
- False positives are allowed; false negatives are forbidden.

Scissor / Opacity / Compositing
- Only record spans for actual writes that pass scissor checks.
- Opacity compositing may produce no net change, but still mark span; false positives are OK.
- When scissor stacks are used, mark spans only for the intersected row segments.

Determinism & Ordering
- Use Vec/SmallVec per row to avoid hash-based ordering.
- Merge order is deterministic: left-to-right; stable tie-breakers for equal x0.

Evidence / Telemetry Fields (proposed)
- dirty_span_rows, dirty_span_total, dirty_span_overflows
- max_spans_per_row, merge_gap, span_coverage_cells
- rows_full_dirty

Failure Modes + Fallbacks
- Excessive fragmentation => full_row fallback
- Span list overflow => full_row fallback
- Resize => re-init to full dirty

Tests (Spec-level)
- Unit: merge behavior (overlap, adjacency, guard band, overflow)
- Property: soundness vs random mutation sets (spans cover all mutated cells)
- Regression: deterministic output for identical mutation sequences
- E2E: span stats logged in JSONL under fixed seed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:30:27.225108113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:16:10.481846217Z","close_reason":"Completed dirty-span spec in docs/spec/state-machines.md section 3.14","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","title":"Implement: WebGPU text rendering (atlas + instanced quads)","description":"Goal

- No key ghosting; no stuck modifiers; focus changes handled correctly.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","title":"Demo: run ftui-demo-showcase in browser via ftui-web + FrankenTerm","description":"Goal
- Run `ftui-demo-showcase` fully client-side in the browser via `ftui-web` + `frankenterm-web` (no xterm.js).

Scope
- Boot, render, navigate, resize, and respond to keyboard + mouse with low latency.
- Any OS-dependent features must either:
  - have explicit web implementations, OR
  - be explicitly disabled with user-visible UX (no silent partial failure).
- Perf + stability: no leaks, no jank, and predictable frame-time under typical demo workloads.

Testing + Logging (Non-negotiable)
- Create a browser "demo sweep" analogous to `tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh`:
  - emits detailed JSONL (screen_id, seed, size, DPR, timings, frame/patch hashes)
  - failures emit minimal repro traces that can be replayed
- Determinism: recorded sessions replay to identical hashes on the same build.

Acceptance Criteria
- A single web page can run the demo for 10+ minutes without runaway memory or degraded responsiveness.
- JSONL logs validate against the shared schema and are rich enough to debug mismatches.
- Seed/time-freeze determinism is preserved end-to-end.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.10","title":"Optimize: ftui-tty reduce poll syscalls during burst input","description":"Goal
- Improve ftui-tty input throughput under bursty workloads (pipes/PTYs, paste storms) without changing event semantics.

Baseline (release)
- hyperfine (test binary): tests::pipe_large_ascii_burst_roundtrips mean ~123.9ms
- strace -c: ~4098 poll + ~4122 read syscalls

Change
- In read_event(), when the event queue is empty and the reader is nonblocking, opportunistically drain any newly-arrived bytes (nonblocking) before returning None.

After (release)
- hyperfine (test binary): mean ~101.9ms (1.22x faster)
- strace -c: ~2 poll + ~539 read syscalls

Isomorphism proof
- Ordering preserved: yes (bytes parsed in-order; we only pull more bytes earlier).
- Tie-breaking unchanged: N/A
- Floating-point: N/A
- RNG: N/A
